pr.title: Harden and relax Firebase upload rules for real-world media MIME compatibility
pr.body:
Uploads were being rejected by Firebase rules in valid client scenarios, especially when devices/browsers emitted MIME variants not covered by current allowlists. This update aligns Firestore metadata validation and Storage object validation so legitimate uploads pass consistently.

- **Rule alignment across Firestore + Storage**
  - Normalized MIME checks to be case-insensitive (`.lower()`) in both `firestore.rules` and `storage.rules`.
  - Kept document payload validation and storage object validation in sync to avoid “uploaded file accepted but document write rejected” mismatches.

- **Expanded accepted media MIME variants**
  - Added support for common mobile/image/video variants:
    - `image/heic`, `image/heif`
    - `video/x-m4v`
  - Applied to announcement media, Sunday service photos, and status/attachment validation paths where relevant.

- **Trusted Storage URL validation update**
  - Updated Firestore trusted URL regex to accept project-scoped Bible Matrix bucket variants (including suffixed environments) while still restricting to Firebase Storage hosts and project bucket naming.

```rules
&& request.resource.contentType is string
&& request.resource.contentType.lower() in [
  'image/jpeg',
  'image/png',
  'image/webp',
  'image/gif',
  'image/heic',
  'image/heif'
];
```
pr.author: Copilot
